description: Educational frontend dev carousels. Two alternating visual styles — gradient motivational and dark technical. High engagement pattern: hook with relatable dev pain point, teach with visuals, close with CTA.

style_a.name: Gradient Motivational
style_a.usage: Identity/personality slides, dev culture, 'be the X not the Y' format
style_a.layout: centered text block with slight glass/frosted overlay card
slide_flow: Slide 1: Hook question — 'Are you the dev they actually commit to... or just another branch they delete?'
Slides 2-5: One 'Be the X, not the Y' statement per slide with matching illustration
Slide 6: Bigger motivational statement
Final slide: CTA — 'Want to become the dev no one can ignore? Visit GreatFrontEnd today'
style_a.copy_formula: Be the [positive dev thing], not the [negative dev thing they [verb]]
examples: Be someone's package.json, not their node_modules — don't be replaceable when it's time to
Be the React component everyone wants to render, not the console.log they ignore
Be the commit that matters, not the merge conflict they ignore at 2 AM
Be the clean CSS, not the div with a hundred inline styles
Be the API they call, not the error 404 they scroll past

style_b.name: Dark Technical Deep-Dive
style_b.usage: Framework comparisons, system design, code walkthroughs, interview prep
style_b.background: #0D0D0D to #1A1A2E — near-black with subtle dark gradient
style_b.text_per_slide: Header + 3-5 bullet points OR header + diagram
style_b.layout: top-aligned header, content below with icons/illustrations
decorative_elements: colored icon badges (red, green, blue circles)
code snippet blocks with syntax highlighting
slide_flow: Slide 1: Bold title + hook question — 'Angular vs React' with tech logos
Slides 2-N: One concept per slide with header + bullets + visual
Code slides: dark code block with syntax highlighting
Final slide: Summary or CTA to GreatFrontEnd platform
style_b.copy_formula:
[Topic] — [Subtitle explaining the angle]
Bullet points with emoji indicators

style_e.name: Black Card / Cards Against Humanity
style_e.usage: Drey's raw voice. Hot takes, controversial opinions, punchy statements. One thought per slide. No decoration — the words ARE the design.
style_e.accent_color: #FFD700 (gold) for key words/numbers only — used sparingly
style_e.font_style: Inter Black, 48-96px depending on slide. Centered. No serif. No handwriting. Clean and heavy.
style_e.text_per_slide: 1-2 sentences MAX. One idea per slide. If you can't say it in 15 words, it's two slides.
style_e.layout: Dead center. Text only. No images, no icons, no decoration. The emptiness IS the design — it forces the reader to sit with the words.
slide_flow: Slide 1: Hook — provocative opening. 'Bro nobody is talking about this.'
Slides 2-5: One fact/take per slide. Build tension. Each slide makes them need the next.
Slide 6: Drey's personal take — this is where the personality hits hardest
Slide 7: CTA — gold @itisdrey, 'save this / send it to someone'
style_e.copy_formula:
[Short punchy statement.]
[Dim subtext that adds context or punchline.]
style_e.why_it_works: Contrast. In a sea of colorful, busy carousels — pure black with white text STOPS the scroll because it's different. The emptiness creates gravity. People read every word because there's nothing else to look at.

style_f.name: Apple Keynote
style_f.usage: Premium presentations. Big reveals, dramatic stats, product-launch energy. When the content deserves the Steve Jobs treatment.
style_f.background: radial-gradient(ellipse at 50% 40%, #1a1a2e 0%, #000 70%) — deep black with subtle blue-ish center glow
style_f.font_style: Inter Black for hero numbers (96-120px), Inter Bold for titles (52-58px), Inter Light for body (24px). Letter-spacing: -2px on heroes, -1px on titles.
style_f.text_per_slide: Hero slides: ONE number or ONE sentence. Feature slides: 3 items max with icons.
style_f.layout: Centered vertically. Generous whitespace. Thin 60px divider lines between sections. Everything breathes.
decorative_elements: Subtle text-shadow glow behind hero text (rgba blue/gold at 0.3 opacity, 80px blur)
Thin horizontal divider lines (60px wide, 2px, 20% white opacity)
Comparison cards with subtle borders (rgba white 0.1)
Feature list with emoji icons left-aligned
style_f.logo_placement: bottom-left, very dim. Slide counter bottom-right in uppercase tracking ('01 / 07')
slide_flow: Slide 1: Dramatic setup — 'What if we told you...' then the big reveal
Slide 2: THE BIG NUMBER — 120px font, glow effect. One stat that shocks.
Slide 3: Feature list — 3 findings with emoji icons, clean layout
Slide 4: Comparison cards — side by side, subtle borders, contrasting colors
Slide 5: The solution — another big number or single sentence
Slide 6: Personal take — Drey's voice, but elevated. Still him, just... premium.
Slide 7: CTA — 'Follow @itisdrey' in Apple blue
style_f.copy_formula:
[Small setup line in dim text]
[MASSIVE reveal in white/colored]
[divider]
[Context in body text]
style_f.why_it_works: The Steve Jobs effect. Massive typography + empty space = every word feels important. The glow effects make it feel premium without being busy. People associate this style with authority and expertise.
style_f.difference_from_black_card: Black Card is RAW — no polish, just words. Keynote is CINEMATIC — same dark background but with glow effects, comparison cards, feature lists, and dramatic reveals. Black Card is a tweet. Keynote is a presentation.

style_d.name: Sketch / Whiteboard
style_d.usage: Drey's FAVORITE. Explainers, 'let me break this down' posts, how things work under the hood, building in public
style_d.background: #FFF9F0 — warm off-white, like notebook paper. Optional: faint grid lines or dot grid
style_d.text_color: #2D2D2D — dark charcoal, like marker on paper
style_d.accent_color: #FF6B35 (orange) for highlights, #4ECDC4 (teal) for secondary, #FF3B3B (red) for X marks / warnings
style_d.font_style: Hand-drawn feel — Caveat, Virgil, or Comic Neue. Headers large and slightly tilted. Body clean but informal
style_d.text_per_slide: Header + hand-drawn diagram or 2-3 bullet points with doodle arrows
style_d.layout: Loose, organic. Not grid-locked. Elements slightly off-center like someone drew it on a whiteboard mid-conversation
decorative_elements: Underlines that look hand-drawn (wavy, not straight)
slide_flow: Slide 1: Hook — hand-drawn title with a question or bold statement. Feels like someone just grabbed a marker
Slides 2-5: One concept per slide with hand-drawn diagram + short explanation
Code slides: code in a 'notepad' style box with hand-drawn border
Final slide: 'Follow @itisdrey for more' in casual handwriting style
style_d.copy_formula:
So basically... [simple explanation]
[hand-drawn diagram showing the concept]
The thing nobody tells you: [insight]
style_d.why_it_works: Feels authentic and personal. Like Drey literally whiteboarded this for you. Low production value = high trust. People save sketch carousels because they feel like notes from a smart friend.
examples: So basically, React re-renders work like this... [arrow diagram]
Nobody explains prop drilling simply. Let me try. [stick figure passing boxes]
Here's what ACTUALLY happens when you type a URL... [hand-drawn flow]

style_c.name: White Technical/Interview
style_c.usage: Interview challenges, system design breakdowns, performance tips
style_c.font_style: Bold serif or sans-serif headers, clean body text
style_c.layout: header top, large visual/diagram center, caption bottom
decorative_elements: blue dot pagination indicators at top
large quotation marks for interview questions
slide_flow: Slide 1: Interview question in big quotes — '"Convert Virtual DOM into real HTML DOM?"'
Slide 2: 'The Core Concept' — explain the fundamental idea
Slides 3-5: Step-by-step breakdown with diagrams
Slide 6: Code implementation
Final: 'Try it yourself' CTA

universal_patterns.pagination_dots: colored dots at top of each slide showing progress (blue=current, gray=remaining)
hook_patterns: Relatable dev pain point as question
'Most devs do X. Here's why Y is better.'
cta_patterns: Start your journey with comprehensive coding prep
Save this for your next interview
engagement_drivers: Dev humor and memes mixed with real knowledge
Each slide is self-contained (works even if they stop scrolling)
Code snippets are real and runnable

carousel_maker_config.note: Use these settings when generating carousels in the carousel-maker skill
carousel_maker_config.words_per_slide: 15-25 for style_a, 30-60 for style_b, 25-40 for style_c
carousel_maker_config.font_family: Inter, SF Pro Display, or similar clean sans-serif
carousel_maker_config.image_gen_prompt_prefix: Minimalist tech infographic slide, clean modern design, 1080x1080,
carousel_maker_config.style_a_prompt: purple-blue gradient background, frosted glass card, bold white centered text, small cartoon developer illustration, modern tech aesthetic
carousel_maker_config.style_b_prompt: dark near-black background (#0D0D0D), bold white header text, red accent highlights, tech icons, code snippet with syntax highlighting, modern developer education style
carousel_maker_config.style_c_prompt: clean white background, bold dark text, blue accent color, architecture diagram, browser mockup, interview prep style, pagination dots at top
